tell me about Leonard Leo.answer: **Leonard Leo** — donor in Dark Money. gave $12K politically · 4 board / affiliation seats
tell me about Leonard Leo.plain_english: **In plain English:** Leonard Leo is a dark-money vehicle — a nonprofit that can legally accept unlimited donations and is not required to publicly disclose who gave. On the books: received $0 from 0 donors, distributed $12K to 1 recipient. The named donors in the list below are the ones the IRS forced into the open through Schedule I grant disclosures by *other* nonprofits. The rest of the money arrived anonymously by design.
bullets: Chairs/sits on: Rule of Law Trust (Trustee, 2020–2024), Marble Freedom Trust (Trustee and Chairman, 2021–2021), Marble Freedom Trust (Trustee and Chairman), Rule of Law Trust (Trustee)
Via Marble Freedom Trust: moved $995M to allies. Support: Schwab Charitable Fund $158M, Schwab Charitable Fund $154M, Schwab Charitable Fund $154M.
Via The 85 Fund: received $556M; moved $202M to allies. Support: Donors Trust $92M, Donors Trust $92M, Donors Trust $12M.
Via Judicial Crisis Network: received $216M; moved $14M to allies. Support: One Nation $9.0M, One Nation $3.0M, American Future Fund $1.0M.
Via Rule of Law Trust: received $153M; moved $80M to allies. Support: Schwab Charitable Fund $24M, Judicial Crisis Network $22M, Schwab Charitable Fund $14M.

breakdown.label: Donors (publicly disclosed)
breakdown.note: US tax law doesn't require 501(c)(4) organizations to disclose who funds them. When you see '0 donors' elsewhere on the site for this entity, that's the law at work, not missing data. Known gifts may be public via news reporting.
